title: Inbound PayStream payment webhook handler
summary: STRUCTURAL EXAMPLE — integration slice type. This file demonstrates the structural shape of an integration slice, including an inbound integration contract with direction, transport, idempotency, timeout, retry policy, failure mapping, and versioning rules. The domain content is fictional and must not be copied.

stakeholder_intent.actor: PayStream Payment Gateway delivering payment lifecycle events to the internal payment service
stakeholder_intent.goal: Keep the internal payment record in sync with the authoritative payment status reported by PayStream
stakeholder_intent.business_value: Enables real-time payment status updates without polling; reduces reconciliation latency from hours to seconds
stakeholder_intent.trigger: PayStream calls the webhook endpoint after a payment transitions to CAPTURED, REFUNDED, or FAILED
stakeholder_intent.success_outcome: The internal Payment record reflects the new status within 5 seconds of the PayStream event; duplicate deliveries are silently acknowledged

in_scope: Receive and authenticate inbound HTTPS POST requests from PayStream
Deduplicate events by eventId and update the internal Payment record status
Map PayStream error conditions to appropriate HTTP response codes
out_of_scope: Outbound calls to PayStream (separate integration slice)

preconditions: A shared HMAC-SHA256 signing key is provisioned in the application secret store
The Payment record identified by paymentId already exists in the local database
PayStream is configured to POST to the registered webhook URL with schema version 2.x

business_rules.rule: Every inbound webhook request must carry a valid HMAC-SHA256 signature computed with the provisioned PayStream signing key
business_rules.applies_when: A POST request arrives at the webhook endpoint
business_rules.outcome: Requests with missing or invalid signatures are rejected with HTTP 401 and no state change is applied

business_rules.rule: Events with a previously processed eventId are silently acknowledged without re-applying state changes
business_rules.outcome: Duplicate deliveries return HTTP 200 immediately; the Payment record is not mutated a second time

inputs.name: X-PayStream-Signature
constraints: HMAC-SHA256 hex digest of the raw request body using the provisioned signing key

inputs.name: X-PayStream-Schema-Version
constraints: Must be 2.x; requests with version 1.x are rejected

inputs.name: eventId
constraints: Globally unique PayStream event identifier; used as the idempotency key

inputs.name: paymentId
constraints: Must match an existing Payment record

inputs.name: amountCents
constraints: Positive integer; authoritative amount for CAPTURED events

inputs.name: occurredAt
constraints: ISO-8601 datetime of the event occurrence at PayStream

outputs.body: Empty body; PayStream interprets any 2xx as successful delivery

errors.message: Request signature does not match; event rejected
PayStream webhook schema version 1.x is no longer accepted
Request body is not valid JSON or is missing required fields

integration_contracts.transport: HTTPS POST with HMAC-SHA256 request signing; TLS 1.2 minimum
integration_contracts.request_contract: JSON body: { eventId: string, paymentId: string, status: CAPTURED|REFUNDED|FAILED, amountCents: integer, occurredAt: ISO-8601 }; header X-PayStream-Signature: HMAC-SHA256 hex; header X-PayStream-Schema-Version: 2.x
integration_contracts.response_contract: HTTP 200 empty body on success or duplicate; HTTP 400 on malformed payload or unsupported version; HTTP 401 on signature failure; never return 5xx to suppress retry loops
integration_contracts.idempotency: Deduplicate by eventId using the processed_events table; if eventId exists, return 200 immediately without applying state changes
integration_contracts.timeout: Handler must respond within 10 seconds; PayStream treats no-response as a delivery failure and schedules a retry
integration_contracts.retry_policy: PayStream retries up to five times with exponential back-off starting at 30 seconds; the handler must remain idempotent across all retries
integration_contracts.failure_mapping: Signature failure → 401 (no retry by PayStream); malformed JSON → 400 (no retry); unsupported schema version → 400 (no retry); duplicate event → 200 (no retry); application error → 200 to prevent retry loop; payment not found → 200 with PAYMENT_NOT_FOUND logged for reconciliation
integration_contracts.compatibility: Accept X-PayStream-Schema-Version 2.x header; reject 1.x with 400 UNSUPPORTED_WEBHOOK_VERSION; forward-compatible for additive 2.x fields using a strict unrecognized-field-ignore strategy

state_changes.precondition: Event passes signature verification and is not a duplicate

changes.value_source: Mapped from PayStream event status: CAPTURED→CAPTURED, REFUNDED→REFUNDED, FAILED→FAILED

security.authentication: Verify the X-PayStream-Signature HMAC-SHA256 header using the signing key from the application secret store before processing the request body
authorization_rules: Only requests from PayStream with a valid HMAC-SHA256 signature are processed
security.data_classification: Payment transaction data; classified as financial PII under internal data policy
audit_requirements: Log eventId, paymentId, resolved status, and processing outcome for every inbound event
Retain webhook audit logs for 90 days to support reconciliation queries

architecture_constraints: Signature verification must run before any database access
Idempotency check and state update must execute in a single database transaction
allowed_changes: Add the webhook handler, HMAC verification guard, idempotency check, and payment status updater
forbidden_changes: Do not expose the signing key outside the HmacVerifier component
Do not issue outbound calls to PayStream from within the webhook handler
dependencies: Reuse HmacVerifier from the existing notification signing module
Reuse ProcessedEventStore from the existing outbox infrastructure
implementation_context.migration_strategy: Add the processed_events table (eventId TEXT PRIMARY KEY, processed_at TIMESTAMPTZ) and the lastEventAt column to payments; both are additive and non-breaking
implementation_context.consistency_and_concurrency: Wrap the idempotency lookup and payment status update in a serializable transaction to prevent a race between concurrent duplicate deliveries

acceptance_scenarios.title: Valid CAPTURED event updates the payment record
given: A Payment record exists with status PENDING
A valid HMAC-SHA256 signature is present on the request
The eventId has not been processed before
when: PayStream POSTs a CAPTURED event to /webhooks/paystream
then: The Payment record status is updated to CAPTURED
The amountCents and lastEventAt fields are updated from the event payload
The eventId is recorded in processed_events

acceptance_scenarios.title: Duplicate event is acknowledged without re-applying state changes
given: The eventId was already processed and recorded in processed_events
A valid signature is present on the duplicate request
when: PayStream delivers the same event a second time
then: The Payment record is not mutated
No duplicate entry is created in processed_events

test_cases: Unit-test HMAC signature verification with valid and tampered payloads
Unit-test idempotency: second call with same eventId returns 200 without state change
Unit-test unsupported schema version header rejection
Integration-test full event processing against a seeded payment database and processed_events table
non_functional_requirements: Handler must respond within 10 seconds under peak payment event load to avoid PayStream delivery timeouts
definition_of_done: Signing key is not logged or exposed outside HmacVerifier

decision_log.decision: Reuse the existing HmacVerifier component for signature validation
decision_log.rationale: HmacVerifier already provides constant-time HMAC comparison and key retrieval from the secret store; reimplementing this would introduce a second code path for the same operation

decision_log.decision: Persist processed eventIds in a dedicated processed_events table rather than relying on the payment record alone
decision_log.rationale: ProcessedEventStore provides a generic idempotency table already used by the outbox consumer; reusing it avoids a bespoke idempotency column on the payments table

decision_log.decision: Return HTTP 200 for application errors (payment not found, mapping failure) to prevent PayStream retry loops
decision_log.rationale: PayStream retries all 4xx and 5xx responses except 401 and 400; returning 200 on application errors prevents retries from amplifying transient failures

codebase_evidence.finding: HmacVerifier.verify performs a constant-time HMAC-SHA256 comparison and loads the key from the application secret store; already used by the notification signing module and confirmed reusable for webhook verification

ProcessedEventStore provides insert-or-ignore semantics on the processed_events table and is used by the outbox consumer to deduplicate domain events; the same table structure suits webhook eventId deduplication
